calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2020 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Thomas Charles Handy and Carl Henry Tandy.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Edward A. Treder, Esq. appeared via CourtCall for plaintiff New Penn Financial LLC, dba Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing, a Delaware limited liability company.
David Rittenhouse, Litigation Foreclosure Manager for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendants Thomas Charles Handy and Carl Henry Tandy.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Reformation, Quiet Title, Specific Performance and Declaratory Relief was filed on January 22, 2019.
Order granting plaintiff leave to name Thomas Charles Handy in place of DOE Number 1, and Carl Henry Tandy in place of DOE Number 2, was filed on May 1, 2019.
Notice of Pendency of Action was filed on May 24, 2019.
Summons, Complaint, DOE Amendments and accompanying documents were served on defendant Thomas Charles Tandy on May 28, 2019.
Substitute service of the Summons, Complaint, DOE Amendments and accompanying documents on defendant Carl Henry Tandy occurred on May 28, 2019 and was effectuated on June 7, 2019.
DOE Amendments were filed with the Clerk's Office on July 18, 2019.
Default as to defendants Thomas Charles Handy and Carl Henry Tandy was entered on August 5, 2019.
Dismissal Without Prejudice as to "All Persons Unknown...," DOES 1 to 10 and Plaintiff's 3rd Cause of Action for Quiet Title was filed on August 16, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff New Penn Financial LLC, dba Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing, a Delaware limited liability company, and against the defaulted defendants Thomas Charles Handy and Carl Henry Tandy, as follows:

The Court hereby reforms rhw Deed of Trust retroactively for the real property located 1370 Thomas Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94124-3306 as set forth in Paragraph 3 of the Judgment, to wit,

"Beginning on the northeasterly line of Thomas Avenue at a point distant southeasterly thereon 150 feet from the southeasterly line of Jennings Street, running thence southeasterly along the northeasterly line of Thomas Avenue 25 feet, thence at a right angle northeasterly 100 feet, thence at a right angle northwesterly 25 feet, thence at a right angle southwesterly 100 feet to the point beginning."

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
